marketState: neutral-bearish with extreme 8-16D weather spike creating near-term short-squeeze risk
summary: Front-month Jul-26 at $3.15 is $0.19 above the fair-value midpoint of $2.96 but well within the $2.46–$3.46 band, making it mildly stretched rather than extreme; by the +21D horizon the fair value rises to $3.07 and price is essentially 'FAIR'. The dominant near-term signal is 8–16D demand running 58.5% above the 5-year average and above the 5-year maximum (rangePos 2.07), a heat extreme that historically suppresses storage injections and is not yet priced into the flat Aug–Oct futures strip ($3.15–$3.19). Managed money is crowded short at the 17th 5-year COT percentile (net –84,909), providing significant short-squeeze fuel if heat-driven storage prints come in below expectations. Structural headwinds remain: storage sits +153 Bcf above the 5-year norm with a recent bearish injection surprise (+25 Bcf vs +48 Bcf forecast), the typical 4-week seasonal move is –2.3% with only 31% probability higher, and seasonality reliability is Low (44pp spread).

longTerm.rationale: Storage +153 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish injection surprise is a persistent structural headwind; the typical 4-week seasonal move is –2.3% with only 31% probability higher and Low reliability (44pp spread). COT net-short at the 17th percentile is a contrarian bullish signal, but at $3.15 — already above the fair-value midpoint of $2.96 — the risk/reward for a committed 3–12-week long is poor until storage data begins narrowing the surplus.
longTerm.invalidation: Two consecutive below-forecast storage injections that demonstrably narrow the +153 Bcf surplus would flip stance to LONG.
longTerm.trigger: Two consecutive below-forecast storage injections narrowing the 5y surplus below ~100 Bcf AND a pullback in front price to the $2.95–$3.05 zone (near fair-value midpoint of $2.96)

COT at the 17th 5-year percentile means any bullish storage surprise could trigger a sharp short squeeze toward the fair-value upper band at $3.46–$3.57; the 1h RSI at 34.9 signals near-term oversold conditions and the 3-week probability-higher reading of 69% supports a rally into the mid-July window.
shortTerm.invalidation: Daily close below $2.97, or 8–16D weather demand model revision back below the 5-year average
